abstract.text: Process a single BGRA32 frame through the SDK pipeline.
Retrieve license information from the SDK (optional).
Assign a name (tag) to a face ID in the tracker.
Learn/improve the current face template (optional helper).
On-device face recognition with fused liveness and TrueDepth anti-spoofing.
Get the current tracker as an in-memory blob.
Load a tracker from an in-memory blob.
Extract one face template from a single still image (no video tracker), for
image-based reference enrolment. Loads + mirrors the 8-bit BGRA buffer like
, detects the largest face with Luxand’s still-image API,
and returns its
bytes (fixed size) so the caller can
append it to a reference bank.
must match the colour order of
the gallery the template will join. A decodable image with no detectable
face is not an error — returns
with
left nil.
Get a device hardware identifier from the SDK (smoke test).
Extract an
for one currently-tracked face, to be retained as an enrolment
reference and later passed back to
for graded matching.
Clear tracker.
Apply tracker parameters from an FRSDK-formatted parameter string.
Activate and initialize the SDK.
Free a tracker.
USE FREE TRACKER when tearing down to re enroll.
Real still-image identity match against a reference bank.
Configure global SDK parameters.
Create a new tracker.
The vendor’s similarity↔FAR calibration (FSDK_GetMatchingThresholdAtFAR):
the FSDK_MatchFaces similarity threshold at which the false-acceptance
rate equals
. The mapping is owned by the vendor and can change
between SDK builds — query it instead of hardcoding similarities.
